chapter_title.en: The Prostration of Gratitude (sujood Al-shukr) and What to Say in it

text: 972 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «مَنْ سَجَدَ سَجْدَةَ اَلشُّكْرِ وَ هُوَ مُتَوَضِّئٌ كَتَبَ اَللَّهُ لَهُ بِهَا عَشْرَ صَلَوَاتٍُ وَ مَحَا عَنْهُ عَشْرَ خَطَايَا عِظَامٍ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.972 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) that He said:
“Whoever performs a prostration of gratitude (Sajdat al-Shukr) while in a state of purity (wudu), Allah (swt) writes for him ten good deeds, removes ten significant sins from him, and grants him ten prayers (blessings).”
